sn47.19:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Sumbhas. Now there is a Sumbhan town named Sedaka. There the Blessed One addressed the monks, “Monks!” “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
sn47.19:1.3: “Once upon a time, monks, a bamboo acrobat, having erected a bamboo pole, addressed his assistant, Frying Pan:
sn47.19:1.4: ‘Come, my dear Frying Pan. Climb up the bamboo pole and stand on my shoulders.’
sn47.19:1.5: “‘As you say, Master,’ Frying Pan answered the bamboo acrobat and, climbing the bamboo pole, stood on his shoulders.
sn47.19:1.6: “So then the bamboo acrobat said to his assistant,
sn47.19:1.7: ‘Now you watch after me, my dear Frying Pan, and I’ll watch after you.
sn47.19:1.8: Thus, protecting each other, watching after each other, we’ll show off our skill, receive our reward, and come down safely from the bamboo pole.’
sn47.19:1.9: “When he had said this, Frying Pan said to him,
sn47.19:1.10: ‘But that won’t do at all, Master.
sn47.19:1.11: You watch after yourself, and I’ll watch after myself,
sn47.19:1.12: and thus with each of us protecting ourselves, watching after ourselves, we’ll show off our skill, receive our reward, and come down safely from the bamboo pole.’
sn47.19:2.1: “What Frying Pan, the assistant, said to her Master was the right way in that case.
sn47.19:2.2: “Monks, the establishing of mindfulness is to be practiced with the thought, ‘I’ll watch after myself.’
sn47.19:2.3: The establishing of mindfulness is to be practiced with the thought, ‘I’ll watch after others.’
sn47.19:2.4: When watching after yourself, you watch after others. When watching after others, you watch after yourself.
sn47.19:3.1: “And how do you watch after others when watching after yourself?
sn47.19:3.2: Through cultivating (the practice), through developing it, through pursuing it.
sn47.19:3.3: This is how you watch after others when watching after yourself.
sn47.19:3.4: “And how do you watch after yourself when watching after others?
sn47.19:3.5: Through endurance, through harmlessness, through a mind of goodwill, & through sympathy.
sn47.19:3.6: This is how you watch after yourself when watching after others.
sn47.19:3.7: “The establishing of mindfulness is to be practiced with the thought, ‘I’ll watch after myself.’
sn47.19:3.8: The establishing of mindfulness is to be practiced with the thought, ‘I’ll watch after others.’
sn47.19:3.9: When watching after yourself, you watch after others. When watching after others, you watch after yourself.”
